value: Measures exposure of assets to flood hazard using hazard intensity and asset value.
physical_risk = hazard * value ; indicator = total_risk / total_value
A high value means the asset, country or sector is strongly exposed to physical climate risk.
A low value means the asset, country or sector is less exposed to physical climate risk.
For a non-expert investor: prefer countries, sectors and assets with the lowest indicator values.
Country graph: compares average physical climate risk across countries. Lower bars are better.
Sector graph: compares average physical climate risk across sectors. Lower bars are better.
Assets table/map: shows which individual assets are the safest or riskiest. Prioritize low-risk assets.
Investment rule: lower indicator = better candidate for investment; higher indicator = more caution needed.
